questions.question: Casualization of workforce would result in:
options: Decrease in the vulnerability of the workforce.
Increase in the vulnerability of the workforce.
Increase in the number of regular workers.
questions.explanation: Casualization refers to an increase in temporary or casual employment, leading to job insecurity and increased workforce vulnerability.

questions.question: Choose the correct chronological sequence in ascending order (earliest to latest): (A) Establishment of NABARD (B) FRBMA (Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act) (C) Third Five Year Plan (D) Nationalization of RBI
questions.explanation: The correct order is: - (D) Nationalization of RBI: 1949 - (C) Third Five Year Plan: 1961–66 1 - (A) Establishment of NABARD: 1982 - (B) FRBMA: 2003.

questions.question: Under flexible exchange rate, when the price of domestic currency in terms of foreign currency increases, it is called:
questions.explanation: In a flexible exchange rate system, when the domestic currency’s value increases relative to foreign currency, it is referred to as appreciation.

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II: List-I List-II (A) Pegged exchange rate system (I) Setting up of IMF and World Bank (B) Managed floating (II) Fixed exchange rate system (C) Bretton Woods Conference (III) Dirty floating (D) Third element in BOP (IV) Error and Omission Account Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - Pegged exchange rate system → Fixed exchange rate system (II). - Managed floating → Dirty floating (III). - Bretton Woods Conference → Setting up of IMF and World Bank (I). - Third element in BOP → Error and Omission Account (IV).

questions.question: implies that resource extraction is not above the rate of regeneration of resources and the wastes generated are within the assimilating capacity of the en- vironment.
questions.explanation: Carrying capacity refers to sustainable resource use without exceeding the environ- ment’s ability to regenerate and absorb waste.

questions.question: Government of India enacted the Right to Education Act in 2009 to make free education a Fundamental Right of all children in the age group of:
questions.explanation: The Right to Education Act guarantees free and compulsory education for children aged 6 to 14 years.

questions.question: Following are the sources of electricity generation in India. Arrange them in correct sequence on the basis of total power generation capacity (higher to lower): (A) Nuclear (B) Thermal (C) New and renewable energy (D) Hydro Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct order based on electricity generation capacity in India is: - (B) Thermal: The largest contributor to electricity generation. - (D) Hydro: The second-largest contributor. - (C) Renewable energy: Third-largest contributor. - (A) Nuclear: Smallest contributor. 4

questions.question: Arrange the following items in correct sequence: (A) Competition Act (B) FEMA (C) FERA (D) MRTP Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The chronological sequence is: - (D) MRTP Act: 1969. - (C) FERA: 1973. - (B) FEMA: 1999. - (A) Competition Act: 2002.

questions.question: The Great Leap Forward (GLF) campaign was initiated in .
questions.explanation: GLF was launched in 1958 by Mao Zedong to accelerate industrialization and col- lectivization in China.

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II: List-I List-II (A) Great Leap Forward (I) Protecting the future generation (B) Mao Zedong (II) Aimed at industrializing the coun- try on a massive scale (C) Brundtland Commission (III) Introduced the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution (D) Our Common Future (IV) Seminal Report that explained sustainable development as meeting the basic needs of all for a better life Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A) Great Leap Forward - Aimed at industrializing the country on a massive scale (II). - (B) Mao Zedong - Introduced the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution (III). - (C) Brundtland Commission - Protecting the future generation (I). - (D) Our Common Future - Seminal report on sustainable development (IV). 6

questions.question: Second phase of Green Revolution is termed from:
questions.explanation: The second phase of the Green Revolution (1970s–1980s) focused on crop diversifi- cation and technological advancements, complementing the initial focus on wheat.

questions.question: During the 1980s, the growth rate in Pakistan improved on account of which of the following reasons?
options: High amount of foreign direct investment inflow
questions.explanation: Pakistan experienced improved economic growth during the 1980s due to favorable agricultural conditions and a good harvest. 7

questions.question: When there is an unexpected rise in the sales, .
options: There will be unplanned accumulation of inventories.
There will be unplanned decumulation of inventories.
questions.explanation: An unexpected rise in sales leads to a faster depletion of inventory than planned, causing an unplanned decumulation.

questions.question: During festive season, the currency deposit ratio .
questions.explanation: During festivals, people tend to withdraw more cash for spending, leading to an increase in the currency deposit ratio.

questions.question: European Monetary Union was created in .
questions.explanation: The European Monetary Union (EMU) was established in 1999, introducing the euro as a common currency for non-cash transactions.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement in the context of Circular Flow of Income in a two-sector economy:
options: Household receives factor services from firms.
Household supplies factor services to Government.
questions.explanation: In a two-sector economy: - Households provide factor services (e.g., labor, capital). - Firms use these factors to produce goods and services.

questions.question: Identify the correct set of statements: (A) Ex Ante Saving represents what is actually planned to save. (B) Ex Post Saving represents what is actually planned to save. (C) Ex Post Saving depicts what has actually happened. 9 (D) Ex Ante Post Saving represents what is actually saved.
questions.explanation: - Ex Ante Saving: Planned savings (Correct). - Ex Post Saving: Actual savings (Correct). - Ex Ante Post Saving: Invalid term (Incorrect).

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II: List-I List-II (A) Annual Financial Statement (I) Create liabilities or reduce financial assets (B) Capital Receipts (II) Trade surplus (C) Capital Payment (III) Main budget document (D) Export > Import (IV) Create financial assets or reduce liabilities Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - Annual Financial Statement: Main budget document (III). - Capital Receipts: Create liabilities or reduce assets (I). - Capital Payments: Create assets or reduce liabilities (IV). - Export > Import: Trade surplus (II).

questions.question: How is civic authority getting benefitted due to biocomposting?
options: They have to dispose increased quantity of waste.
They don’t have to dispose any waste.
Because civic authority has no role in waste management.
They have to dispose reduced quantity of waste.
questions.explanation: Biocomposting reduces the volume of waste requiring disposal, helping civic author- ities. 11

questions.question: Choose the correct statement concerned with the passage:
options: Earthworm takes equal time to convert organic matter into compost with respect to normal composting process.
Earthworm takes more time to convert organic matter into compost with respect to normal composting process.
Earthworm takes less time to convert organic matter into compost with respect to normal composting process.
Comparison between earthworm-based composting and normal composting cannot be made.
questions.explanation: The passage highlights that earthworms accelerate the composting process compared to traditional methods.

questions.question: Water bodies including ground water system have suffered due to:
options: Chemical contamination and increased demand for irrigation
questions.explanation: The passage highlights that the extensive use of chemical fertilizers has led to chemical contamination of water bodies and an increased demand for irrigation. 12

questions.question: In certain parts of the country, cattle are maintained only because they:
options: Produce milk to increase income of farmers.
Produce dung which is an important fertilizer and soil conditioner.
Are a symbol of traditional ethics and values.
questions.explanation: In certain parts of India, cattle are primarily maintained for their dung, which acts as a natural fertilizer and soil conditioner.

questions.question: What should be the suitable title for the above passage?
questions.explanation: The passage primarily discusses biocomposting, focusing on its benefits as an eco- friendly alternative to chemical fertilizers.

questions.question: Before the British rule, India was well known for which manufacturing indus- tries’ products?
options: Cotton and Silk or Khadi textiles
Cotton and Silk textiles, metal and precious stones, etc.
questions.explanation: Before British rule, India was globally renowned for its high-quality handicrafts in textiles (cotton and silk) and metal/precious stone works. 14

questions.question: What kind of interests was the colonial government concerned with in the economic policy for Indian industries?
options: To promote the handicraft industries of India
Promote and protect small scale industries of India
Protect and promote the economic interests of Indian industry along with their home coun- try
Provide protection and promotion of the economic interests of their home country
questions.explanation: The colonial government focused on safeguarding Britain’s economic interests, rather than fostering Indian industrial growth.

questions.question: Choose the economists who estimated India’s National Income and Per Capita Income:
options: Dadabhai Naoroji, William Digby, Bernier, V.K.R.V. Rao, and R.C. Desai
Dadabhai Naoroji, William Digby, Findlay Shirras, V.K.R.V. Rao, and R.C. Desai
Dadabhai Naoroji, Jagdish Bhagwati, V.K.R.V. Rao, and R.C. Desai
Dadabhai Naoroji, Findlay Shirras, and V.K.R.V. Rao
questions.explanation: These economists made significant contributions to estimating India’s national in- come and per capita income during the colonial era. 15

questions.question: What was the country’s growth of aggregate real output during the first half of the 20th century?
options: Less than two percent aggregate growth and one percent per capita output growth.
Less than two percent aggregate growth and 1.5 percent per capita output growth.
Less than 2.5 percent aggregate growth and 0.5 percent per capita output growth.
Less than two percent aggregate growth and 0.5 percent per capita output growth.
questions.explanation: India’s aggregate real output growth during the colonial period was less than 2

questions.question: According to the passage, which of the following statement was true about Indian industries’ products?
options: Indian products were of inferior quality and cheap in the worldwide market.
Indian products were promoted by the colonial government and competed with other coun- tries’ markets also.
Indian products enjoyed a worldwide market based on quality of material used and the high standard of craftsmanship.
Indian products’ raw material for primary goods was imported from other countries and it helped to increase export.
questions.explanation: Indian handicrafts were globally famous for their superior materials and craftsman- 16 ship before British rule.

questions.question: is a market where economic agents can freely exchange their endow- ments with each other.
questions.explanation: A market economy allows economic agents to freely trade goods, services, and resources based on supply and demand.

questions.question: Which of the following are correct statements? (A) Marginal Utility is the change in Total Utility due to consumption of one additional unit of commodity. (B) Two Indifference Curves intersect each other. (C) Marginal Utility becomes Zero at a level when Total Utility remains constant. (D) Diminishing Marginal Rate of Substitution does not affect Indifference Curve. (E) Indifference Curve slopes downwards from left to right. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A) Correct: Marginal Utility is the change in Total Utility from consuming one more unit. - (C) Correct: Marginal Utility = 0 when Total Utility is at its maximum. - (E) Correct: Indifference Curves slope downward to maintain equal utility.

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II: List-I (Characteristic) List-II (Implication) (A) Equilibrium (I) Plans of all the consumers and firms in the market match (B) Excess supply (II) Demand decreases with an increase in income (C) Inferior good (III) Supply is greater than market de- mand (D) Price ceiling (IV) Imposition of upper limit by gov- ernment Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A) Equilibrium - (I): At equilibrium, the plans of all consumers and firms in the market match. 18 - (B) Excess supply - (III): Excess supply occurs when supply exceeds market demand. - (C) Inferior good - (II): An inferior good is characterized by demand decreasing as income increases. - (D) Price ceiling - (IV): A price ceiling is imposed by the government to set an upper price limit.

questions.question: When Elasticity of Demand Curve is 1 at every point on the Demand Curve, this curve is known as:
questions.explanation: A rectangular hyperbola indicates that the elasticity of demand is constant at 1 at all points, meaning the percentage change in price equals the percentage change in quantity demanded.

questions.question: According to the Theory of Consumer Behaviour, inequality p1x1 + p2x2 ≤ M is called the Consumer’s:
questions.explanation: The inequality p1x1 + p2x2 ≤ M represents the consumer’s budget constraint, where M is the income, and p1, p2 are the prices of goods x1 and x2.

questions.question: Choose the correct statements from the following: (A) Adam Smith is termed as Father of Modern Economics. (B) J.M. Keynes gave the Theory of Income, Output, and Employment. (C) Four Sector Model = C + I + G + (X − M). (D) Store of value is primary function of money. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A): Correct. Adam Smith is regarded as the Father of Modern Economics. - (B): Correct. Keynes introduced the Theory of Income, Output, and Employment. - (C): Correct. The four-sector model is accurately represented. - (D): Incorrect. The primary function of money is a medium of exchange, not a store of value.

questions.question: Rearrange the given statements in proper chronological sequence in ascending 20 order (earliest to latest): (A) General Theory of Employment, Interest, and Money by Keynes. (B) Mahalanobis was made a fellow of Britain’s Royal Society. (C) An Inquiry into the Nature and Cause of the Wealth of Nations by Adam Smith. (D) The Economic Consequences of the Peace by Keynes. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (C) Smith’s Wealth of Nations: Published in 1776. - (D) Keynes’s Economic Consequences: 1919. - (A) Keynes’s General Theory: 1936. - (B) Mahalanobis became Royal Society Fellow: 1945.

questions.question: If there is no government and no foreign trade, then:
questions.explanation: Without government or foreign trade, government expenditure ( G), taxes ( T ), im- ports (M), and exports ( X) are all zero. 21

questions.question: The value of Nominal GDP is $1100 and the value of Real GDP is $1000. Find the value of GDP deflator:
questions.explanation: The GDP deflator is calculated as: GDP Deflator = Nominal GDP Real GDP = 1100 1000 = 1.1.

questions.question: Identify the correct statements: (A) Stocks are defined over a period of time. (B) Flows are defined over a period of time. (C) Flows are defined at a particular point of time. (D) Stocks are defined at a particular point of time. (E) GVA at factor costs + Indirect Taxes – Subsidies = GVA at market prices. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (B): Correct. Flows are measured over time (e.g., income). - (D): Correct. Stocks are measured at a point in time (e.g., wealth). - (E): Correct. GVA at market prices = GVA at factor costs + indirect taxes – subsidies.

questions.question: If the supply of final goods is assumed to be infinitely elastic at constant price over a short period of time, Aggregate output is determined solely by the value of Aggregate demand. This is called:
questions.explanation: The Keynesian principle of Effective Demand applies when supply is perfectly elastic, so output is determined entirely by aggregate demand.

questions.question: Nominal Interest Rate =
questions.explanation: Nominal Interest Rate is calculated as: Nominal Interest Rate = Real Interest Rate + Inflation Rate .

questions.question: Value of MPS (Marginal Propensity to Save) is increased from 0.4 to 0.5. What would be the impact on Multiplier?
options: The size of the multiplier would be increased.
The size of the multiplier would remain the same.
Value of multiplier is undetermined with the given information.
The size of the multiplier would be decreased.
questions.explanation: The multiplier (k) is inversely related to the MPS: k = 1 MPS If MPS increases from 0.4 to 0.5, the multiplier decreases: k1 = 1 0.4 = 2.5, k 2 = 1 0.5 = 2.

questions.question: Paradox of Thrift means:
options: If all the people of the economy increase the proportion of income they save, the total value of investment in the economy will not increase, it will either decline or remain unchanged.
If all the people of the economy increase the proportion of income they spend, the total value of savings in the economy will not increase, it will either decline or remain unchanged.
If all the people of the economy decrease the proportion of income they save, the total value of savings in the economy will not increase, it will either decline or remain unchanged. 24
If all the people of the economy increase the proportion of income they save, the total value of savings in the economy will not increase, it will either decline or remain unchanged.
questions.explanation: The Paradox of Thrift explains how increased savings by everyone in the economy reduces consumption, leading to decreased aggregate demand, lower income, and hence reduced total savings.

questions.question: depicts what has actually happened:
questions.explanation: Ex-Post reflects the actual outcomes or results, as opposed to Ex-Ante, which refers to planned or forecasted values.

questions.question: If the value of Investment Multiplier is 5 and the increased income is 800 crore in an economy, then find the value of change in the investment in the economy:
questions.explanation: The Investment Multiplier (k) is given by: k = ∆Y ∆I Where: - k = 5, - ∆ Y = 800 crore. Rearranging for ∆ I: ∆I = ∆Y k = 800 5 = 160 crore.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true? (A) Quantitative tools control the extent of money supply by changing the CRR. (B) There are two types of open market operations – outright and upright. (C) A fall in the bank rate can decrease the money supply. (D) Selling of a bond by RBI leads to reduction in quantity of reserves. (E) The RBI can influence money supply by changing the rate at which it gives loans to com- mercial banks. Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A): Correct. CRR is a quantitative tool to regulate money supply. - (B): Incorrect. Open market operations include outright and repo transactions, not upright. - (C): Incorrect. A fall in the bank rate increases money supply. - (D): Correct. Selling bonds absorbs liquidity, reducing reserves. - (E): Correct. RBI can alter money supply by changing the repo rate. 26

questions.question: Match List-I with List-II: List-I List-II (A) Bank Rate (I) Securities are pledged in order to repurchase (B) Marginal Standing Facility (II) Minimum rate at which funds are provided for long term (C) Repo Rate (III) Also known as Penal Interest Rate (D) Reverse Repo Rate (IV) Central Bank borrows funds from commercial banks Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: - (A) Bank Rate: Minimum rate for long-term lending (II). - (B) MSF: Penal interest rate (III). - (C) Repo Rate: Securities pledged for repurchase (I). - (D) Reverse Repo Rate: Central Bank borrows from banks (IV).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a function of Central Bank?
options: It controls the money supply in the economy through different rates.
It acts as a banker to the government.
It accepts deposits and gives loans to people.
It issues the currency of the country.
questions.explanation: The Central Bank does not deal with retail banking functions like accepting deposits or lending to individuals.

questions.question: Article 112 deals with:
questions.explanation: Article 112 of the Indian Constitution pertains to the Union Budget, which is the annual financial statement of estimated receipts and expenditures of the government.

questions.question: All those elements which create liability and decrease the assets of government are known as:
questions.explanation: Capital Receipts include borrowings and liabilities that create obligations for the government and decrease its net worth, such as loans and the sale of assets.

questions.question: Arrange the following elements in correct chronological sequence (earliest to latest): (A) Smithsonian Agreement (B) Bretton Woods Conference (C) Establishment of WTO (D) Gold Standard Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.explanation: The correct chronological order is: - (D) Gold Standard: Prevalent before World War II, up until the 1930s. - (B) Bretton Woods Conference: Held in 1944, establishing the Bretton Woods system and institutions like the IMF and World Bank. - (A) Smithsonian Agreement: Signed in 1971 as an attempt to revise the Bretton Woods system. - (C) Establishment of WTO: Founded in 1995, replacing GATT. 29
